completed_tasks.name: Multi-user data isolation — users/sessions tables, session_id cookie, all queries scoped by user_id
Fix DB migration — drop legacy account_id tables, recreate with user_id INTEGER FK
Fix auth redirect — new users go to / not /no-project (route not in React Router)
Add try/except + logging to OAuth callback DB block — catches DB exceptions, logs traceback
Add step-by-step logging to ALL OAuth callback error paths (step 1 token exchange, step 2 /me fetch, step 3 accessible-resources)

remaining_tasks.name: Account 2 attempts login → check docker logs for 'OAuth step X' error line → fix root cause
Verify sync — add Jira project → no 'Sync failed'
UAT: two browser profiles, different accounts → confirm isolated project lists
UAT: Bug Report Word (.docx) and Sprint Word (.docx) exports
Responsive UI polish UAT: sidebar animations, card layouts, navbar logo

blockers.description: Account 2 login fails with 'Authorization failed — please try again.' — exact step unknown until fresh attempt with new step logging active
blockers.workaround: New logging active in backend. Account 2 attempts login → immediately run: powershell.exe -Command "docker logs group2_warnnoi300percent-backend-1 --tail 15 2>&1" → look for 'OAuth step 1', 'OAuth step 2', 'OAuth step 3', or 'OAuth callback DB/session error' line.

human_actions_pending.context: auth.py has +4 lines of step logging — uncommitted

human_actions_pending.action: Account 2 attempts login at http://localhost:3000 → immediately run docker logs --tail 15
human_actions_pending.context: Backend has full step logging. The log will reveal exactly which OAuth step fails for account 2.

decisions.decision: Proper users+sessions tables with INTEGER user_id FK on all data tables
decisions.rationale: Codebase refactored to this model. database.py migration drops+recreates legacy account_id tables on startup.

decisions.decision: session_id cookie (opaque token) not jira_session (account_id value)
decisions.rationale: Proper session abstraction. session_id → user_id lookup in sessions table.

decisions.decision: Add logger.error to ALL OAuth callback exception handlers
decisions.rationale: Account 2 from same Atlassian org keeps failing. Logging added to step 1 (token exchange), step 2 (/me fetch + no account_id), step 3 (accessible-resources), and step 4 (DB/session). Every failure path now produces a log line identifying the exact step.

next_action: 1. git add backend/routers/auth.py && commit. 2. Account 2 logs in. 3. Immediately check docker logs --tail 15. 4. Find 'OAuth step X' line. 5. Fix that step.
context_notes: Account 2 is from the SAME Atlassian organization as account 1. Both should be able to use the OAuth app. Account 1 (ps@manaosoftware.com, user_id=1) has MLO + SAM projects, works fine. Account 2 has never successfully logged in. The 'Authorization failed' error = token_exchange_failed redirect, which now comes from 4 different error paths. Backend currently running with WatchFiles-detected reload of new auth.py (step logging active). DB schema is correct (user_id=True, account_id=False on all 4 data tables).
